turns.content: What brings you in today?

I've been having a fever that keeps coming and going for four days now, and honestly it's wearing me out. The worst part is the chills at night—they hit so hard I can't get comfortable or sleep properly. And my whole body just aches, like I've been run over.
When did this start?

It started about four days ago, on Tuesday evening after I got home from work. I just felt achy and tired, and then the fever kicked in that night. It's been coming in waves ever since—sometimes I feel okay during the day, but the evenings are always worse.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?

It's mostly in my muscles and joints—my lower back, my legs, and even my shoulders feel like they're all knotted up and sore. It's not a sharp pain, more like a deep, dull ache that makes me want to just lie still. And when the chills come, it's like my whole body is shaking and tensing up at the same time, which makes the soreness even worse.
Have you had any fever or chills?

Yes, definitely. The fever spikes to about 101 or 102°F at night, and that's when the chills really hit hard—I'll be shivering under a blanket even though my skin feels hot. During the day it might drop a bit, but I still feel warm and uncomfortable.
What medications are you currently taking?

I've just been taking some over-the-counter ibuprofen for the body aches and fever. It helps a little, but the symptoms come back once it wears off. I haven't taken anything else—no prescription meds or herbal stuff.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?
No, I don't have any chronic conditions. I'm generally pretty healthy—no diabetes, high blood pressure, or anything like that. This kind of thing is unusual for me.
Are you allergic to any medications?
I'm not allergic to anything that I know of. I've taken ibuprofen before without any issues, and I've had antibiotics in the past without a problem. So I don't think allergies are a concern here.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?

Just the ibuprofen every six hours or so, and I've been trying to rest as much as possible. I've also been drinking a lot of water and tea, but honestly nothing seems to shake it completely. I haven't tried any other remedies or seen anyone else about it.
Does anything make it better or worse?

Resting and lying down definitely help take the edge off the body aches. But moving around—like getting up to use the bathroom or just walking to the kitchen—makes the soreness flare up. And the chills are worse at night no matter what I do, which is really frustrating.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It's really thrown off my routine. I've had to call in sick for the past two days because I can't focus or stand for long periods in the classroom. Even at home, I'm just lying around—I don't have the energy to cook or even watch TV properly. I'm usually pretty active, so this has been a big disruption.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
I don't smoke at all—never have. I do have a drink maybe once a week, like a beer or glass of wine with dinner, but I haven't had anything since this started because I just don't feel up to it.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?
Not that I know of. My wife and kids are all healthy, and no one at work has mentioned being sick like this. I don't think anyone in my family has had these exact symptoms recently.
Have you experienced anything like this before?

No, I've never had anything quite like this before. I've had the occasional cold or mild flu, but nothing with this level of fever, chills, and full-body aching that lasts for days. It's really been out of the ordinary for me.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?

Physically, I'd say I'm at about an 8 right now—the aches and chills have been relentless, and I'm just exhausted from not sleeping well. Emotionally, maybe a 4 or 5—I'm not panicking, just feeling kind of worn down and frustrated that this isn't letting up. I'm hoping you can help me figure out what's going on so I can get back to normal.
Is there anything else you think I should know?

I think that covers everything. I did forget to mention that I've also had a mild headache off and on, mostly in the mornings, and I've lost my appetite—I've barely eaten anything solid in the past couple of days. Other than that, I think I've told you all the important stuff. Thanks for listening.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

Honestly, I'm just trying to push through day by day. I keep telling myself it's probably just a bad virus and it'll pass, but the nights are the hardest—lying awake shivering really tests your patience. I've been leaning on my wife to bring me tea and check in, which helps, but I'm ready for this to be over.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I'm just taking it one day at a time, really. Resting as much as I can, staying hydrated, and trying not to worry too much. My wife's been great about checking on me and making sure I eat little bits here and there. It's frustrating, but I'm hoping it's just a stubborn bug that'll run its course soon.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I guess I'm just trying to stay positive and not let it get to me too much. I've been keeping a bit of a routine—resting, drinking fluids, and taking the ibuprofen when I need it. My wife's been a big help, and that keeps me from feeling completely alone in this. Otherwise, I'm just waiting for it to pass.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I appreciate you asking—I think I've been handling it okay, all things considered. I'm not one to complain much, so I've just been pushing through the discomfort and trying to stay hopeful. It helps knowing I'm here talking to you now—makes me feel like I'm finally doing something about it instead of just waiting it out.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I think I've covered that already—I'm just resting, staying hydrated, and relying on my wife for support. I'm not really anxious about it, just worn out and ready to feel better. Is there anything else you need to know to figure out what's going on?
